msg.description: Global variable providing information about the current message call including sender address, value sent, calldata, and gas remaining for the transaction execution context.

sender.description: Returns the address of the account that directly called the current function.

value.description: Returns the amount of wei sent with the current message call.

data.description: Returns the complete calldata payload of the current message call.

sig.description: Returns the first four bytes of the calldata, representing the function selector.

block.description: Global variable providing access to current block properties including timestamp, number, difficulty, gas limit, and coinbase address for on-chain time and block-dependent logic.

timestamp.description: Returns the current block's Unix timestamp in seconds since epoch.

number.description: Returns the current block number in the blockchain.

chainid.description: Returns the chain ID of the current blockchain network.

coinbase.description: Returns the address of the miner or validator that produced the current block.

basefee.description: Returns the base fee of the current block according to EIP-1559.

tx.description: Global variable providing transaction-level properties including the original sender (origin) and gas price, representing the outermost transaction context in the call chain.

origin.description: Returns the address of the externally-owned account that initiated the entire transaction chain.

gasprice.description: Returns the gas price of the current transaction in wei.

address.description: The address type holds a 20-byte Ethereum address with members for checking balance, sending ether, and performing low-level calls to other contracts on the blockchain.

balance.description: Returns the balance of the address in wei.

transfer.description: Sends the specified amount of wei, reverting on failure with a fixed 2300 gas stipend.

send.description: Sends wei and returns false on failure instead of reverting, with a 2300 gas stipend.

call.description: Performs a low-level call to the address, forwarding all available gas and returning success status and return data.

delegatecall.description: Performs a delegate call that executes the target's code in the caller's storage context.

code.description: Returns the bytecode deployed at the address, empty for externally-owned accounts.

mapping.description: Hash table data structure that maps keys to values with O(1) lookup, where all possible keys exist and map to a default zero value, not iterable or enumerable by design.

require.description: Validates a condition and reverts the transaction with remaining gas refunded if the condition evaluates to false, optionally providing a descriptive error message string.

revert.signature: revert() | revert(string memory reason) | revert CustomError(args)
revert.description: Aborts execution and reverts all state changes in the current call context, optionally providing a reason string or custom error with typed parameters for gas-efficient errors.

emit.description: Triggers a named event that stores arguments in the transaction log, enabling off-chain applications to listen for and react to on-chain state changes through indexed topics.

payable.description: Modifier that enables a function to receive Ether or converts a regular address to a payable address, required for any contract function that accepts native currency transfers.

keccak256.description: Computes the Keccak-256 cryptographic hash of the input bytes, widely used for generating unique identifiers, verifying data integrity, and implementing Merkle tree structures.

abi.description: Provides ABI encoding and decoding functions for serializing function call parameters, computing function selectors, and packing data for cryptographic hashing and cross-contract calls.

encode.description: ABI-encodes the given arguments with padding to 32-byte boundaries.

encodePacked.description: Non-standard packed encoding without padding, commonly used before hashing.

decode.description: Decodes ABI-encoded data according to the given type tuple.

encodeWithSelector.description: ABI-encodes arguments prepended with the 4-byte function selector.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
